commit.message:
Update site content — March 6, 2026 (#4)

Add missing commands and features from recent releases:
- /copy command (v0.0.422) - copy last response to clipboard
- /chronicle command (v0.0.419) - show session timeline
- /tasks command (v0.0.407) - manage background tasks
- /changelog command (v0.0.406) - view release notes
- --output-format json flag (v0.0.422) - JSONL output mode
- --plugin-dir flag (v0.0.421) - load local plugins

Add new YouTube video:
- 'Copilot CLI in VS Code' by Visual Studio Code (52s, March 5, 2026)

All commands and flags verified against official release notes.
Version baseline remains at v0.0.422 (March 5, 2026).

Co-authored-by: GitHub Copilot Workflow <copilot-workflow@github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Update constraint tickets: sequential animation→physics pipeline

Replace binary kinematic/dynamic model with sequential pipeline:
animation solver always runs → target poses become physics motor targets
→ motor_strength (0.0–1.0) controls animation vs physics authority per bone.

Dynamic ragdoll bodies are still driven by animation + IK every tick.
Physics enforces physical plausibility on top of animation targets.

commit.message:
feat(CVE-2020-25084): add xHCI usb_packet_copy assertion PoC

- Add CVE-2020-25084/ directory with full exploit and README
- Root cause: xhci_setup_packet() ignores return value of xhci_xfer_create_sgl()
  and usb_packet_map(); when DATA TRB has direction mismatch, SGL is destroyed
  (nsg=0), usb_packet_map returns 0 but p->iov.size=0, then usb_packet_copy
  asserts (0+18<=0) -> SIGABRT
- Key discovery: unmapped PA (0xDEAD…) does NOT trigger because QEMU's
  address_space_map() uses bounce buffers for non-RAM regions (non-NULL return)
- Real trigger: DATA TRB with OUT direction (TRB_TR_DIR=0) for an IN transfer
  causes xhci_xfer_create_sgl direction-mismatch -> xhci_die -> empty SGL
- Update SKILL (reproduce-qemu-cve.md) with xHCI MMIO pattern, bounce buffer
  quirk, NEC xHCI SS_FIRST port layout, and HCSPARAMS1 field encoding
- Rename SKILL file from reproduce.md to reproduce-qemu-cve.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(docs): fix Mermaid syntax error in Configuration System diagram

Nodes with paths starting with '/' were interpreted as Mermaid
parallelogram shape syntax [/text/], breaking when the path contained
internal slashes (e.g. /config/config.json, /data/options.json).
Fixed by quoting the labels with double quotes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
hotspot: split raw chart, fix event-driven scraping, add dashboard validation

Dashboard:
- Split the 6-series raw metrics chart into separate LTE and NR charts
- Switch x-axis labels from wall-clock time to elapsed seconds
- Remove redundant scrape-age and cell-data-age from status bar
- Draw chart axes/labels on initial load (no more blank canvases)

Scraper:
- Fix Playwright event dispatch: replace queue.get() (which starved
  Playwright's sync event loop) with page.expect_response() so model.json
  updates from the SPA are captured at their native ~2s rate instead of
  only on the 10s fallback poll.

Validation:
- Add scripts/validate-dashboard.py: headless Playwright check for JS
  console errors, canvas rendering, and expected DOM elements
- Document in README alongside existing sanity-check.ps1

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix doc paths and passkey tracking after system updates (#1066)

* Fix broken doc links, passkey last_used, and rent coverage month

- Update all help/guide doc paths in layout_admin.html, layout_student.html,
  admin_insurance.html, admin_store.html, and admin_payroll.html from the old
  hyphenated format (e.g. teacher-students) to the new slash-separated directory
  structure (e.g. teacher/students) following the doc system revamp.

- Fix passkey 'Never used' always displayed: credentials are stored with
  credential_id=NULL (managed by Passwordless.dev), so the credential_id-based
  lookup in passkey_auth_finish never matched. Now updates last_used on all
  credentials for the authenticated admin/sysadmin on successful sign-in.

- Fix rent coverage month label rolling over to next month for end-of-month due
  dates (e.g. Jan 31 + 1 day = Feb 1). Coverage label is now derived directly
  from payment.coverage_month and payment.coverage_year, which already store
  the correct billing period.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01QyQhUoPvdd46rHUhJVhbq6

Create rpg-ywes subtasks: unified constraint system with physics integration

10 subtasks for Custom Skeletal Mesh Format with Pose Constraints:

1. rpg-qslo: Unified constraint type system (20 Blender-compatible types)
2. rpg-1blk: Space conversion and constraint evaluation core
3. rpg-s9ob: IK solvers (CCD, FABRIK, Spline IK)
4. rpg-augx: Transform copy and tracking constraints
5. rpg-9sjh: Limit constraints (rotation, location, scale)
6. rpg-ngt5: Transform mapping, action, child-of, pivot
7. rpg-pkgw: Surface/volume constraints (floor, clamp, shrinkwrap)
8. rpg-j8n0: Physics engine integration (ragdoll, bidirectional)
9. rpg-b17x: .fskel format and loader
10. rpg-v00q: Visual test battery (8 tests)

Key design decision: constraints are a UNIFIED system shared by both
the animation pose solver and physics rigid-body engine. Same
constraint_def_t drives both animation bones and physics bodies.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: specify linux/amd64 platform for Docker build

The container was experiencing 'exec format error' because the Docker
image was built without explicitly specifying the target platform.
This caused architecture mismatch when running on AKS nodes.

Added --platform linux/amd64 flag to ensure the image is built for
the correct architecture compatible with AKS Linux nodes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: don't cache rate-limit failures as permanent negative results

- Only cache undefined (non-existence) for 404 responses in
  listRepoDirectory. Transient errors like 403 rate-limit or 500 server
  errors are no longer cached, allowing the next request to retry.
- Don't create bogus fallback items for directory-like paths when
  expandGroupDirectoryReference fails. Previously a rate-limited
  directory listing would produce a single phantom 'skills' item.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: consolidate publish workflow — npm, pages, release on merge to master

- build: install, test, build, upload dist artifact
- publish-npm: download dist, publish to npmjs.com
- deploy-pages: download dist, deploy to GitHub Pages (parallel with publish-npm)
- release: create/update GitHub Release with dist assets and
  auto-generated notes (runs after publish-npm succeeds)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Fix TiltCard RAF never stopping — now self-stops when animation reaches rest
- Fix share button clipboard fallback for browsers without Clipboard API

commit.message:
chore: add .gitleaks.toml to allowlist doc placeholders

The curl-auth-header rule flags the example `curl -H "Authorization:
Bearer YOUR_PASSWORD"` command in docs/troubleshooting.md as a secret.
This is a false positive — YOUR_PASSWORD is a placeholder, not a real
credential. Allowlist the path to suppress the finding.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
MYTUBE-196 Test: Access dashboard while unauthenticated — user redirected to login (#173)

* MYTUBE-196 test: automate Test: Access dashboard while unauthenticated — user redirected to login

* MYTUBE-196: Add auth guard to dashboard page component

Move auth protection from DashboardContent to the page level to ensure
the route is protected before rendering. This prevents unauthenticated
users from seeing the dashboard layout momentarily before the client-side
redirect completes.

- Convert DashboardPage to a client component
- Check user authentication state before rendering dashboard
- Redirect to login if user is not authenticated
- Prevents flash of unprotected content on static deployments

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

re-adjust the position without having to re-click the feature bar.

commit.message:
Add bind-pose computation and visual humanoid test

- Add gltf_scene_compute_bind_pose() to gltf_loader API
  Walks cgltf joint hierarchy to compute joint_world × IBM matrices
  Uses cgltf_node_transform_world() (column-major, no transpose needed)

- New file: src/renderer/gltf/gltf_bind_pose.c (1 public function)

- Add 3 unit tests for bind-pose computation (17/17 pass):
  compute_bind_pose, compute_bind_pose_null, compute_bind_pose_small_buf

- New visual test: tests/visual/p004_visual_humanoid.c
  Loads humanoid.glb, creates 12 skeletal meshes with skinning shader
  Custom GLSL 430 shader matching skeletal_mesh attribute layout (6/7)
  Raw SSBO bone palette with bind-pose matrices
  Orbit camera, 90 frames at 30fps, video capture + PPM snapshots

commit.message:
Update rollback.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[Feature] Add Debian (.deb) packaging support for FE and BE (#68821)

Why I'm doing:
Currently, StarRocks installation on Debian-based systems (Ubuntu/Debian) relies on manual tarball extraction and manual environment configuration. This is error-prone and does not follow the Linux Standard Base (LSB) hierarchy. Providing native .deb packages simplifies deployment ensures consistent directory structures across clusters and enables modern process management via systemd.

What I'm doing:
Introduced a Debian packaging infrastructure for both Frontend (FE) and Backend (BE) components.

Implementation Details:

Standardized Path Mapping:

Binaries & Libraries: Moved to /usr/lib/starrocks/ to keep the application isolated.
Configuration: Relocated to /etc/starrocks/ and registered as conffiles to ensure user settings persist through package upgrades.
Logs: Directed to /var/log/starrocks/.
Metadata/Data: Directed to /var/lib/starrocks/.
Automated Lifecycle Management: * Added a postinst script to automate the creation of a dedicated starrocks system user/group.

Automated directory permission handling (chown/chmod) for log and data paths.
Systemd Integration: * Created starrocks-fe.service and starrocks-be.service units.

Configured services to run as the starrocks user with appropriate resource limits (LimitNOFILE).
Integrated --daemon to allow systemd to track process health correctly.
Build Automation: Provided a cross-platform build.sh script (compatible with macOS/BSD and Linux/GNU sed) to generate the .deb files from the official build output.

Fixes #68205

Signed-off-by: sunghj1118 <sunghj1118@gmail.com>
Signed-off-by: Hyunjoon Sung <60638615+sunghj1118@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: move behavioral styles into custom element static initializers

Each element now self-registers its required CSS into document.head on
first class evaluation, guarded by getElementById for idempotency.

HTMLInclude: html-include display/content-visibility + body fade-in
FEComponent: fe-component { display: none }
FECompose:   fe-compose { display: contents }

style.css and shell-layout.css retain only site-level rules.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: split GUI_APP.py into modular app/ package structure

- app/core/logger.py: logging setup and log_function decorator
- app/core/excel_logger.py: ExcelLogger class for Excel report generation
- app/ssh/ssh_handler.py: SSH_setup class for SSH connections
- app/ssh/worker.py: Worker QThread for SSH command execution
- app/ssh/console_worker.py: SshConsoleWorker for interactive SSH shell
- app/ssh/scp_worker.py: ScpWorker for SFTP file transfers
- app/dialogs/remote_file_browser.py: RemoteFileBrowserDialog
- app/widgets/terminal_widget.py: TerminalWidget for SSH console display
- app/ui/main_window.py: TestStationInterface main window
- main.py: application entry point
- GUI_APP.py: compatibility shim preserving all public names

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add status filter bar and extend fetch window to 180 days

Add a collapsible filter bar above the Eisenhower matrix with per-status
toggles (All / Today / Hide). Defaults: new, inProgress, blocked show
all; finished and cancelled show today only. Extend lookback window from
30 to 180 days to recover items that already fell off.

Closes #13

fix: add child nav — move child-add into (parent) group to bypass routing guard

router.push('/(setup)/child-add') was immediately overridden by the
routing guard (hasFamily=true → redirect to /(parent)). Moving the
screen to /(parent)/child-add fixes the navigation.
